title: melica ciliata subsp. transsilvanica
intro: Melica ciliata subsp. transsilvanica, also known as Transylvanian melic grass, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Melica ciliata subsp. transsilvanica include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves and stems to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
